subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Place and Value Detectives
objective: Identify the place and value of a digit.
introduction: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Place and Value Detectives”.
A key word used in the lesson “Place and Value Detectives”.
A key word used in the lesson “Place and Value Detectives”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: In 4,352, what is the value of 3?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. 30 and 3 do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Place and Value Detectives”?

options.text: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.
The correct response is “3 in 4,352 is hundreds” because it follows the lesson explanation: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Calling place and value identical” because it follows the lesson explanation: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

items.prompt: In 4,352, what is the value of 3?
items.explanation: The correct response is “300” because it follows the lesson explanation: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “5 is in the tens place” because it follows the lesson explanation: Place names a position; value is what the digit represents in that position.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Place and Value Detectives; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Place and Value Detectives.
